text: If people weigh the arguments properly and have empathy for the victims, people will be more inclined to favor capital punishment.
original_text: However , if people weigh the arguments properly , and have empathy for the victims , they will be more inclined to favor capital punishment .

text: According to a study conducted in the late 1960s, there was a 7% crime rate increase in the years when the death penalty law was abolished.
original_text: According to a study conducted in the late 1960 ’s , there was a 7 % crime rate increase on the years when this law was abolished .

text: Capital punishment is not excessive for people who knowingly and intentionally commit premeditated murder.
original_text: Capital Punishment is not excessive , unnecessary punishment , for those who knowingly and intentionally commits murder in premeditation , to take lives of others .

text: Capital punishment is not an unnecessary punishment for people who knowingly and intentionally commit premeditated murder.
original_text: Capital Punishment is not excessive , unnecessary punishment , for those who knowingly and intentionally commits murder in premeditation , to take lives of others .

text: It is fair that people who violate other people's lives are brought to justice.
original_text: Since they violate other people ’s lives , it is but fair that they are brought to justice and suffer the fate they rightfully deserve .

text: Call your state legislature representatives today to enforce the death penalty in your state!
original_text: Call your state legislature representatives today to enforce the death penalty in your state !

text: The possibility that an execution method may cause pain, whether by accident or as an unavoidable consequence of death, does not meet the standard of an "objectively intolerable risk of harm" required to be considered cruel and unusual.
original_text: PRO : " Simply because an execution method may result in pain , either by accident or as an inescapable consequence of death , does not establish the sort of ' objectively intolerable risk of harm ' [ quoting the opinion of the Court from Farmer v. Brennan , 511 U. S. 825 , 842 , 846 ( 1994 ) ] that qualifies as cruel and unusual ... Kentucky has adopted a method of execution believed to be the most humane available , one it shares with 35 other States ... Kentucky 's decision to adhere to its protocol can not be viewed as probative of the wanton infliction of pain under the Eighth Amendment ... Throughout our history , whenever a method of execution has been challenged in this Court as cruel and unusual , the Court has rejected the challenge .

text: Kentucky has adopted an execution method that is regarded as the most humane currently available and is also used by thirty‑five other states.

text: Kentucky's decision to continue using its execution protocol cannot be taken as evidence of wanton infliction of pain prohibited by the Eighth Amendment.

Kentucky 's decision to adhere to its protocol can not be viewed as probative of the wanton infliction of pain under the Eighth Amendment ... Throughout our history , whenever a method of execution has been challenged in this Court as cruel and unusual , the Court has rejected the challenge .

text: In U.S. legal history, whenever a method of execution has been challenged before the Supreme Court as cruel and unusual, the Court has consistently rejected the challenge.
original_text: PRO : " Simply because an execution method may result in pain , either by accident or as an inescapable consequence of death , does not establish the sort of ' objectively intolerable risk of harm ' [ quoting the opinion of the Court from Farmer v. Brennan , 511 U. S. 825 , 842 , 846 ( 1994 ) ] that qualifies as cruel and unusual ... I believe life is sacred , therefore , one who takes a life should have his own life taken away , too .

text: A person who takes a life should have the taker's own life taken away as well.
original_text: I believe life is sacred , therefore , one who takes a life should have his own life taken away , too .

text: The legal system does not provide adequate representation to the poor in cases where a life hangs in the balance, so it does not provide equal justice under the law.
original_text: How can we contend that we provide equal justice under the law when we do not provide adequate representation to the poor in cases where a life hangs in the balance ?

text: The notion of an eye for an eye, or a life for a life, is a simplistic one.
original_text: The notion of an eye for an eye , or a life for a life , is a simplistic one which our society has never endorsed .

text: Death penalty is a form of revenge.
original_text: Death penalty is a form of revenge .

text: The death penalty is clearly more expensive than a system handling similar cases with a lesser punishment.
original_text: Or to put it differently , the death penalty is clearly more expensive than a system handling similar cases with a lesser punishment .
